title: Classify Heim-PC wide listeners
goal: Classify and, where appropriate, narrow wide Heim-PC listeners including Unifi, Sunshine, GNOME Remote Desktop, Leitstand and Caddy.

approval.note: User authorized full audit remediation. Stop only proven unmanaged previews; change Leitstand runtime only after reviewed code, green CI, preserved unit/log evidence and loopback HTTP rollback proof.

acceptance.assertion: Every listener on 0.0.0.0 or * has an owner, purpose, expected network scope and exposure boundary.
Unmanaged preview servers are either stopped or moved behind explicit local-only/dev-only commands.
Any port binding or firewall change is made only after preserving required LAN/Tailnet functions.

Leitstand server code defaults to loopback and rejects wildcard binding unless an explicit unsafe-wide-bind acknowledgement is present; focused tests cover default, explicit LAN IP, invalid host and wildcard acknowledgement.
The live Leitstand user service is rebuilt from merged main, explicitly configured for 127.0.0.1, and post-restart proof shows HTTP success on loopback with no wildcard listener on port 3000.
Ports 4173 and 4174 are closed with process/unit provenance and clean-repository evidence; no parent tmux shell or worktree is deleted.

metadata.live_audit_finding: unmanaged commonworld http.server on 0.0.0.0:4173 was stopped; remaining wide listeners need policy classification.
metadata.scope_update_20260711: Live audit identified unmanaged Commonworld and merged-Weltgewebe preview listeners on 4173/4174 plus Leitstand contract drift on *:3000. The preview listeners were closed without deleting shells/worktrees; Leitstand requires a repo-bound safe-default patch and runtime proof.
